name: Pizza Hat Crust
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle yeast over warm water and stir in the sugar.
2. Let stand about 5 minutes or until very bubbly.
3. Combine the remaining ingredients with about half of the flour, beating to a smooth batter. Beat in the yeast mixture and then work in the remaining flour with a sturdy spoon until you can toss it lightly on a floured surface and knead it until it feels elastic in texture. The kneading may require about 2/3 cup additional flour, which you will coat your hand with as you knead the dough. Don't let the dough become too stiff and yet don't want it to stick to your hands.
4. Place the dough in a large plastic food bag, spraying inside with Pam or wiping the inside with oil. Place the ball of kneaded dough in this to rise until doubled in bulk. Ensure the plastic bag is large enough that it will permit the dough to double without splitting the bag.
5. When dough has doubled, punch it down and shape it to fit two 15-inch round pizza pans that have been greased and dusted with cornmeal.
6. Add the sauce and topping ingredients exactly in the order listed above, spreading each ingredient evenly over the dough.
7. Let this rise about 20 minutes in a warm place and then bake at 450°F, about 20 to 25 minutes, putting one pizza at a time on the center rack of the preheated oven. If you don't want a pizza-pizza and can only handle one, wrap the second pizza in foil or plastic and seal it with tape to lock out all air. Freeze it before it rises the last time. After letting it thaw 30 minutes, you can bake as directed above.
